settings_item_max_versions_per_file_desc: The maximum number of historical versions kept for each file. Older versions are cleaned up once the limit is exceeded.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_source: The largest uploaded ZIP source file allowed to enter online extraction. Lower values reduce central-directory memory pressure and decompression workload.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_stagin: The largest temporary disk footprint allowed while extracting a ZIP archive, counted as downloaded source archive bytes plus extracted staging bytes before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_uncomp: The largest total uncompressed file bytes accepted inside one ZIP archive before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entrie: The maximum number of ZIP entries accepted during online extraction. This limits metadata-heavy archives with many tiny files or directories.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_files_: The maximum number of regular file entries accepted inside one ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_direct: The maximum number of directory paths accepted or implicitly created while extracting one ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_depth_: The deepest normalized ZIP entry path accepted during online extraction.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_path_b: The maximum UTF-8 byte length accepted for a normalized ZIP entry path.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_compre: The maximum total uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for a ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entry_: The maximum per-file uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for ZIP entries.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_durati: The longest wall-clock runtime allowed for one online extraction task before the server stops it.
settings_item_archive_preview_enabled_de: Master switch for read-only ZIP manifest preview. Keep this off unless you explicitly want the server to scan ZIP central directories for previews.
settings_item_archive_preview_user_enabl: Allow signed-in users to preview ZIP manifests for personal and team files. The master ZIP preview switch must also be enabled.
settings_item_archive_preview_share_enab: Allow public share pages to preview ZIP manifests after password and share-scope checks. Keep this off if shared archive metadata should stay private.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_source: The largest source ZIP file accepted for read-only preview. Lower values reduce temp-file, central-directory, and metadata scanning pressure.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_entrie: The maximum number of ZIP central-directory entries accepted for read-only preview.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_manife: The largest serialized manifest returned to clients. Larger manifests are truncated before response and cache storage.
settings_item_archive_preview_max_durati: The longest wall-clock runtime allowed for one read-only ZIP preview scan.
settings_item_archive_build_max_entries_: The maximum number of expanded file and directory entries allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_total_so: The maximum total source file bytes allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_temp_byt: The maximum estimated or actual ZIP output bytes allowed while creating archives, limiting temporary disk and streaming resource use.
settings_item_thumbnail_max_source_bytes: The largest source file size still allowed to enter thumbnail generation. Larger files are skipped so oversized originals do not overload thumbnail jobs.
settings_item_media_processing_registry_: Enable the media processors you want to use, bind extensions when needed, and let the AsterDrive built-in pipeline act as the final fallback when it is enabled.
settings_item_avatar_dir_desc: Local filesystem directory used for uploaded avatar files. Absolute paths are used as-is. Relative paths are resolved under the server's ./data directory.
settings_item_avatar_max_upload_size_byt: The largest avatar file users are allowed to upload. Oversized uploads are rejected, and requests are still bounded by the server's hard payload limit.
